ranked_movies.title: The Notebook
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a classic, top-tier romance and a benchmark for the genre. Cited multiple times as a favorite and a must-watch.

ranked_movies.title: La La Land
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a high-quality, modern musical romance, though noted for its bittersweet ending. Mentioned in the context of great break-up movies.

ranked_movies.title: 500 Days of Summer
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as a definitive and relatable modern romance film, especially for its portrayal of a non-traditional relationship and break-up.

ranked_movies.title: Me Before You
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a comfort movie and a top recommendation for those seeking heartfelt, emotional romance stories.

ranked_movies.title: True Romance
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated request thread. A foundational 90s film that users are actively seeking similar recommendations for, indicating its high regard.

ranked_movies.title: The Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a light, cute, and enjoyable modern rom-com, often cited as a feel-good watch.

ranked_movies.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a well-liked post-2005 rom-com with a great cast and sharp comedy, fitting the request for modern, quality romance.

ranked_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as the quintessential thoughtful and artistic break-up movie, praised for its unique take on love and memory.

ranked_movies.title: Moulin Rouge
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a great, dramatic musical romance, suitable for both feeling giddy and for a good cry after a break-up.

ranked_movies.title: When Harry Met Sally
ranked_movies.reason: A classic rom-com frequently listed among the best of the genre, known for its sharp dialogue and exploration of friendship turning into love.

ranked_movies.title: You've Got Mail
ranked_movies.reason: Often recommended alongside other Meg Ryan/Tom Hanks classics as a quintessential and comforting 90s romance.

ranked_movies.title: The Princess Bride
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a user's weekly watchlist with a perfect 10/10 score, celebrated as a timeless fantasy adventure romance.

ranked_movies.title: Scott Pilgrim vs. The World
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its blend of college-age romance, sharp comedy, and action, matching the specific request for a movie with 'Andrew Garfield + Emma Stone energy'.

ranked_movies.title: Kalifornia
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated request thread. Users are seeking films with a similar dark, 90s thriller/romance vibe, indicating its cult status.

ranked_movies.title: The Summer I Turned Pretty
ranked_movies.reason: A TV series frequently discussed and highly rated (10/10) by users in the romancemovies subreddit, indicating its popularity and quality within the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Wrong Paris
ranked_movies.reason: A recent Netflix movie that sparked significant discussion. Reactions were mixed but generally positive, with many finding it charming and entertaining.

ranked_movies.title: My Oxford Year
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in multiple threads as a recent watch and a recommendation, fitting the request for newer, heartfelt romance films.

ranked_movies.title: Past Lives
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a top-tier, critically acclaimed modern romance film for those seeking something more artistic and profound.

ranked_movies.title: Natural Born Killers
ranked_movies.reason: The third film in the foundational trio (with True Romance and Kalifornia) that users are seeking similar recommendations for, cementing its discussed status.

ranked_movies.title: The Gorge
